volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 362 - Section 525)

sections.label: Section 362
sections.original: σιγῶ · πέραινε δʼ ὧν σʼ ἀνιστορῶ πέρι .
sections.translation: I am dumb ; proceed with that which touches my inquiry .

sections.label: Section 363
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν ὃ κάμνει τοῦ λόγου μάλιστά σοι ;
sections.translation: Dost know the one weak point in this thy story ?

sections.label: Section 364
sections.original: τί δʼ οὐκ ἐκείνῃ τῇ ταλαιπώρῳ νοσεῖ ;
sections.translation: ’ Tis all weak in that poor lady ’ s case .

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: πῶς ὁ θεὸς ὃ λαθεῖν βούλεται μαντεύσεται ;
sections.translation: How should the god declare that which he wishes hidden ?

sections.label: Section 366
sections.original: εἴπερ καθίζει τρίποδα κοινὸν Ἑλλάδος .
sections.translation: He must , if here upon the tripod he sits for all Hellas to seek to .

sections.label: Section 367
sections.original: αἰσχύνεται τὸ πρᾶγμα · μὴ ʼξέλεγχέ νιν .
sections.translation: He is ashamed of the deed ; do not question him .

sections.label: Section 368
sections.original: ἀλγύνεται δέ γʼ ἡ παθοῦσα τῇ τύχῃ .
sections.translation: Aye , but his victim has her sorrows too .

sections.label: Section 369
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν ὅστις σοι προφητεύσει τάδε .
sections.translation: There is none who will act as thy medium in this .

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: ἐν τοῖς γὰρ αὑτοῦ δώμασιν κακὸς φανεὶς
sections.translation: For were Phoebus in his own temple proved a villain , he would justly wreak his vengeance on the man who expounded to thee his oracles ; desist then , lady ; we must not prophesy against the god ’ s will ,

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: εἰ τοὺς θεοὺς ἄκοντας ἐκπονήσομεν
sections.translation: for it would be the height of folly in us , were we to try and make the gods against their will declare reluctant truths either by sacrifice of sheep at their altars , or by omens from birds . For those answers we strive to extort from heaven , lady , are goods that bring no blessing on our getting ;

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ἃ δʼ ἂν διδῶσʼ ἑκόντες , ὠφελούμεθα .
sections.translation: but what they freely offer , thereby we profit .

sections.label: Section 381
sections.original: πολλαί γε πολλοῖς εἰσι συμφοραὶ βροτῶν ,
sections.translation: Many are the chances that befall the many tribes of men , and diverse are their forms . But scarce one happy scene canst thou find in all the life of man .

sections.label: Section 384
sections.original: ὦ Φοῖβε , κἀκεῖ κἀνθάδʼ οὐ δίκαιος εἶ
sections.translation: Ah ! Phoebus , here as there , art thou unjust

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ἐς τὴν ἀποῦσαν , ἧς πάρεισιν οἱ λόγοι ·
sections.translation: to that absent sufferer , whose cause I now am pleading . Thou didst not preserve thy child , as in duty bound , nor wilt thou , for all thy prophetic skill , answer his mother ’ s questioning , that , if he be no more , a mound may be raised o ’ er him , or , if he live , he may some day be restored to his mother ’ s eyes .

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐᾶν χρὴ τάδʼ , εἰ πρὸς τοῦ θεοῦ
sections.translation: In vain is this the home of oracles if the god prevents me from learning what I wish to ask . But lo ! I see my noble lord , Xuthus , nigh at hand , returning 278 from the lair of Trophonius ; say nothing ,

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: σίγα πρὸς ἄνδρα , μή τινʼ αἰσχύνην λάβω
sections.translation: sir , to my husband of what I have told thee , lest I incur reproach for troubling about secrets , and the matter take a different turn to that which I sought to give it . For women stand towards men in a difficult position , and the virtuous from being mingled with the wicked amongst us are hated ;

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: μισούμεθʼ · οὕτω δυστυχεῖς πεφύκαμεν .
sections.translation: such is our unhappy destiny .

sections.label: Section 401
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν ὁ θεὸς τῶν ἐμῶν προσφθεγμάτων
sections.translation: First to the god all hail ! for he must receive the first-fruits of my salutation , and next all hail to thee , my wife ! Has my delay in arriving caused thee alarm ?

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: οὐδέν γʼ · ἀφίκου δʼ ἐς μέριμναν . ἀλλά μοι
sections.translation: By no means ; but thou art come at an anxious time .

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: λέξον , τί θέσπισμʼ ἐκ Τροφωνίου φέρεις ,
sections.translation: Tell me what response thou bringest from Trophonius , touching our future hopes of mutual offspring .

sections.label: Section 407
sections.original: οὐκ ἠξίωσε τοῦ θεοῦ προλαμβάνειν
sections.translation: He deigned not to forestal the prophecies of Phoebus . This only did he say , that neither thou nor I should return unto our house childless from the shrine .

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: ὦ πότνια Φοίβου μῆτερ , εἰ γὰρ αἰσίως
sections.translation: Majestic mother of Phoebus , to our journey grant success , and may our previous dealings with thy son now find a better issue !

sections.label: Section 413
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ · ἀλλὰ τίς προφητεύει θεοῦ ;
sections.translation: It will be so ; but who acts as the god ’ s spokesman here ?

sections.label: Section 414
sections.original: ἡμεῖς τά γʼ ἔξω , τῶν ἔσω δʼ ἄλλοις μέλει ,
sections.translation: I serve outside the shrine , others within ,

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: οἳ πλησίον θάσσουσι τρίποδος , ὦ ξένε ,
sections.translation: who stand near the tripod , even the noblest of the Delphians chosen by lot , sir stranger .

sections.label: Section 417
sections.original: καλῶς · ἔχω δὴ πάνθʼ ὅσων ἐχρῄζομεν .
sections.translation: ’ Tis well ; I have attained the utmost of my wishes . I will go within ; for I am told that a victim has been slain in public before the temple for strangers ,

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: κοινὸν πρὸ ναοῦ · βούλομαι δʼ ἐν ἡμέρᾳ
sections.translation: and to-day ,— for it is a lucky day ,— I would fain receive the god ’ s oracle . Do thou , my wife , take branches of laurel , and seated at the altars pray to the gods that I may carry home from Apollo ’ s shrine an answer that bodeth well for offspring .

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ , ἔσται . Λοξίας δʼ ἐὰν θέλῃ
sections.translation: All this shall be . Now , at any rate , if Loxias would retrieve his former sins , e ’ en though he cannot be my friend entirely , yet will I accept whate ’ er he deigns to give , because he is a god .

sections.label: Section 429
sections.original: τί ποτε λόγοισιν ἡ ξένη πρὸς τὸν θεὸν
sections.translation: Why doth this stranger lady hint dark reproaches against the god

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: κρυπτοῖσιν αἰεὶ λοιδοροῦσʼ αἰνίσσεται ;
sections.translation: unceasingly , either out of affection for her on whose behalf she seeks the oracle , or maybe because she is hiding something needing secrecy ? Yet what have I to do with the daughter of Erectheus ? She is naught to me .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: πρόχοισιν ἐλθὼν εἰς ἀπορραντήρια
sections.translation: No , I will go to the laver , and from golden ewers sprinkle the holy water . Yet must I warn Phoebus of what is happening to him ; he ravishes a maid and proves unfaithful to her , and after secretly begetting a son leaves him to die . O ! Phoebus , do not so , but as thou art supreme ,

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: ἀρετὰς δίωκε . καὶ γὰρ ὅστις ἂν βροτῶν
sections.translation: follow in virtue ’ s track ; for whosoever of mortal men transgresses , him the gods punish . How , then , can it be just that you should enact your laws for men , and yourselves incur the charge of breaking them ? Now I will put this case , though it will never happen .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: δίκας βιαίων δώσετʼ ἀνθρώποις γάμων ,
sections.translation: Wert thou , wert Poseidon , and Zeus , the lord of heaven , to make atonement to mankind for every act of lawless love , ye would empty your temples in paying the fines for your misdeeds . For when ye pursue pleasure in preference to the claims of prudence , ye act unjustly ; no longer is it fair

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: λέγειν δίκαιον , εἰ τὰ τῶν θεῶν καλὰ
sections.translation: to call men wicked , if we are imitating the evil deeds of gods , but rather those who give us such examples . [ Exit Ion

sections.label: Section 1.452
sections.original: σὲ τὰν ὠδίνων λοχιᾶν
sections.translation: On thee I call , Athena mine , at whose birth-throes no kindly goddess lent her aid ,

sections.label: Section 1.455
sections.original: Προμηθεῖ Τιτᾶνι λοχευ-
sections.translation: delivered as thou wert by Titan Prometheus from the forehead of Zeus . Come , O lady Victory , come to the Pythian shrine , winging thy way from the gilded chambers of Olympus

sections.label: Section 1.460
sections.original: πταμένα πρὸς ἀγυιάς ,
sections.translation: to the city ’ s streets , where Phoebus at his altar on the centre of the world brings his oracles to pass beside the dance-encircled tripod ;

sections.label: Section 1.465
sections.original: σὺ καὶ παῖς ἁ Λατογενής ,
sections.translation: come , too , thou daughter of Latona , together come , ye virgin goddesses , fair sisters of Phoebus ! And be this your prayer , fair maidens , that the ancient house of Erechtheus

sections.label: Section 1.470
sections.original: γένος εὐτεκνίας χρονίου καθαροῖς
sections.translation: may obtain by clear oracles the blessing of children , though late it come .

sections.label: Section 1.472
sections.original: ὑπερβαλλούσας γὰρ ἔχει
sections.translation: For this brings to man a settled source of all-surpassing bliss ,

sections.label: Section 1.475
sections.original: τέκνων οἷς ἂν καρποτρόφοι
sections.translation: even to such as see in their ancestral halls a splendid race of strong young parents blest with offspring , to inherit from their sires

sections.label: Section 1.480
sections.original: ἑτέροις ἐπὶ τέκνοις .
sections.translation: their wealth in due succession after other children ; yea , for they are a defence in time of trouble , and add a charm to weal , affording to their fatherland a saving help in battle .

sections.label: Section 1.485
sections.original: ἐμοὶ μὲν πλούτου τε πάρος
sections.translation: Give me before the pomp of wealth or royal marriages the careful nurture of noble children . The childless life I do abhor , and him who thinks it good I blame ;

sections.label: Section 1.490
sections.original: μετὰ δὲ κτεάνων μετρίων βιοτᾶς
sections.translation: to a happy life amongst my children , blest with moderate wealth , may I hold fast .

sections.label: Section 492
sections.original: ὦ Πανὸς θακήματα καὶ
sections.translation: Ye haunts of Pan , and rocks hard by the grots of Macrae ,

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: ἵνα χοροὺς στείβουσι ποδοῖν
sections.translation: where Agraulos ’ daughters three trip it lightly o ’ er the green grass-lawns before the shrine of Pallas , to the music of the piper ’ s varied note ,

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: ὕμνων , ὅτʼ ἀναλίοις
sections.translation: what time thou , Pan , art piping in those caves of thine , where a maiden once that had a child by Phoebus , unhappy mother ! exposed her babe , forced issue of her woful wooing ,

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: θηρσί τε φοινίαν δαῖτα , πικρῶν γάμων
sections.translation: for birds to tear and beasts to rend , a bloody banquet ! Never have I seen it told in woven tale or legend that children born to gods by daughters of earth have any share in bliss .

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: πρόσπολοι γυναῖκες , αἳ τῶνδʼ ἀμφὶ κρηπῖδας δόμων
sections.translation: Attendant maids , that watch and wait your mistress here at the steps of the temple fragrant with incense , say , hath Xuthus already left the holy tripod and the sanctuary , or doth he still abide within to ask yet further of his childlessness ?

sections.label: Section 514
sections.original: ἐν δόμοις ἔστʼ , ὦ ξένʼ · οὔπω δῶμʼ ὑπερβαίνει τόδε .
sections.translation: He is still in the temple , sir , nor hath he passed this threshold yet .

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: ὡς δʼ ἐπʼ ἐξόδοισιν ὄντος , τῶνδʼ ἀκούομεν πυλῶν
sections.translation: But hark ! I hear a footstep at the outlet of the door , and lo ! thou mayst see my master this moment coming out .

sections.label: Section 517
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , χαῖρʼ · ἡ γὰρ ἀρχὴ τοῦ λόγου πρέπουσά μοι .
sections.translation: All hail ! my son ; that word suits well as my first greeting to thee .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: χαίρομεν · σὺ δʼ εὖ φρόνει γε , καὶ δύʼ ὄντʼ εὖ πράξομεν .
sections.translation: ’ Tis well with me ; do but restrain thyself , and then both of us will be happy .

sections.label: Section 519
sections.original: δὸς χερὸς φίλημά μοι σῆς σώματός τʼ ἀμφιπτυχάς .
sections.translation: Give me thy hand to grasp , thy body to embrace .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: εὖ φρονεῖς μέν ; ἤ σʼ ἔμηνε θεοῦ τις , ὦ ξένε , βλάβη ;
sections.translation: Art thou in thy senses , sir , or hath some spiteful god reft thee of them ?

sections.label: Section 521
sections.original: σωφρονῶ · τὰ φίλταθʼ εὑρὼν οὐ φυγεῖν ἐφίεμαι .
sections.translation: I am in my senses , for I have found what I hold most dear , and am eager to show my love .

sections.label: Section 522
sections.original: παῦε , μὴ ψαύσας τὰ τοῦ θεοῦ στέμματα ῥήξῃς χερί .
sections.translation: Cease ! touch me not , nor tear these garlands of the god !

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original: ἅψομαι · κοὐ ῥυσιάζω , τἀμὰ δʼ εὑρίσκω φίλα .
sections.translation: I will embrace thee , for I am not seizing what is not my own , but only finding my own that I love full well .

sections.label: Section 524
sections.original: οὐκ ἀπαλλάξῃ , πρὶν εἴσω τόξα πλευμόνων λαβεῖν ;
sections.translation: Hands off ! or thou shalt feel an arrow pierce thy ribs .

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: ὡς τί δὴ φεύγεις με ; σαυτοῦ γνωρίσας τὰ φίλτατα —
sections.translation: Why dost thou shun me , now that thou findest in me thy nearest and dearest ?
